content:
# Seismic Refraction

**Summary**: A geophysical method used to determine the depth to bedrock or changes in rock properties using seismic waves.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] applied seismic-refraction to map the boundaries of the aquifer and study acoustic properties.

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used SR to measure seismic wave velocities related to elastic properties and fluids.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] used seismic-refraction to identify saturated zones in sabkhas and sand dunes.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used SR to measure seismic wave velocities related to elastic properties and fluids.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] used seismic-refraction to identify saturated zones in sabkhas and sand dunes.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Seismic refraction was conducted to investigate the acoustic properties of the Khobar Member. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 16]])

- The case study in the Dammam Dome demonstrated the effectiveness of the combined optimization approach in accurately characterizing the subsurface geology using SR data. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 17]])

- The method is effective for identifying fully saturated layers but fails to specify partial amounts of water without additional modeling. ([[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023, p. 25]])
